events.text: Linda Jackson, 53-year-old White female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Invasive ductal carcinoma of the Left breast. Clinical staging: T3N2M1, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C50.911. Stage IV HER2-positive breast cancer with liver and bone metastases.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Bone scan performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Bone scan performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 alopecia. Counseled on expected hair regrowth post-treatment.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 decreased LVEF. LVEF decreased to 48% on echocardiogram. Cardiology consulted. ACE inhibitor initiated.
Bone scan performed for restaging after maintenance HER2-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for results review after maintenance HER2-directed therapy.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for restaging after second-line HER2-directed ADC.
Clinical visit for results review after second-line HER2-directed ADC.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Linda Jackson
MRN: patient_54d553ee3508
Specimen ID: S2023-94120
Date of Procedure: 2023-04-23
Date of Report: 2023-04-26

SPECIMEN: Left breast, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Invasive ductal carcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.6 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
ER: Negative (0%)
PR: Negative (0%)
HER2 IHC: 3+ (positive)
Ki-67: 45%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Linda Jackson - Left breast" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 5.6 x 3.9 x 2.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Linda Jackson
MRN: patient_54d553ee3508
Date of Study: 2023-04-23

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the axial skeleton consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 6 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions as described.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Breast Surgery

Patient: Linda Jackson
MRN: patient_54d553ee3508
Date: 2023-04-26
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Jackson is a 53-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, IV. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IV HER2-positive breast cancer with liver and bone metastases. Pathology confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- type 2 diabetes mellitus

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Office manager
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No significant family history.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- Metformin 1000 mg BID

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dizziness, mild peripheral edema, mild dyspnea on exertion, constipation. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 134/86, HR 66, RR 16, Temp 98.6F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 81.0 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.0, Hgb 11.1, Plt 299, Cr 1.0

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
53-year-old female with IV invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. initial oncology consultation.

1. Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Breast Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Linda Jackson
MRN: patient_54d553ee3508
Date of Report: 2023-05-03
Specimen: Left breast (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.9 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  ERBB2 Amplification (copy number 12) - Amplification [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PIK3CA p.H1047R - Missense (Exon 20)
  TP53 p.Y220C - Missense (Exon 6)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CHEK2 p.I157T - VUS
  PALB2 p.L939W - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - ERBB2 amplification: FDA-approved HER2-directed therapies include trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DXd, tucatinib.
  - PIK3CA H1047R: Alpelisib may be considered in HR+/HER2- setting.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 451x coverage.
Tumor content: 37%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

Mr./Ms. Jackson is a 53-year-old female with IV invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient reports feeling well overall. Weight is stable.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: abdominal bloating, intermittent headaches, constipation, intermittent nausea. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 120/69, HR 69, RR 17, Temp 97.5F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 59.8 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.3, Hgb 14.9, Plt 251, Cr 1.17

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
53-year-old female with IV invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Invasive ductal carcinoma - cycle 2 assessment
   - Continue current treatment regimen
   - Tumor markers trending favorably
2. Symptom management
   - Fatigue management discussed
3. Follow-up in 3 weeks

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Linda Jackson
MRN: patient_54d553ee3508
Date of Study: 2024-07-02

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 8 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: 3 hypodense lesions consistent with metastases, largest in segment 6 measuring 2.6 cm. Decreased in size.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Multiple lytic osseous lesions involving pelvis and bilateral iliac bones consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Linda Jackson
MRN: patient_54d553ee3508
Date of Study: 2024-11-13

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the axial skeleton consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 10 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.
3. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
